Please refer to the Biological Sciences and Chemistry department agreements and an advisor.  <strong style=\"color: rgb(78, 14, 80);\">Group 8 and Group 10</strong>: Please note there may be an applicable PHY 001AB series articulation agreement. Please refer to the Physics department agreements and an advisor.</p>","groupId":"d4d57145-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":9,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"<p>The major in <a href=\"https://local-resources.ucdavis.edu/local_resources/docs/catalog/GenCat20252026.pdf\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">Environmental Policy Analysis & Planning</a> develops skills for designing and assessing sustainable policies for environmental quality and natural resource management.</p><p><br></p><p>This major provides students with a strong background in policy analysis, including the evaluation of policy alternatives and the study of factors affecting policy formulation and implementation. Key components of this interdisciplinary training include a general background in the natural sciences relevant to environmental policy, economics, political science, statistics, and research methodology to quantitatively analyze environmental problems and policy options. In addition, students are encouraged to develop substantive knowledge in a specific field of environmental policy, such as urban and regional planning, water policy, transportation and energy, climate policy, or conservation management.</p>","groupId":"dbd57145-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":1,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"<p>Environmental policy analysis & planning graduates are prepared for employment in environmental, natural resource, energy, and transportation focused public agencies, consulting firms, non-governmental organizations, and businesses, or as legislative aides for elected representatives. The major is also excellent preparation for students who want to go on to graduate work in law, planning, public policy, political science, economics, or business.</p>","groupId":"e4d57145-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":3,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"<p>This major is not currently screening for major preparation at the time of admission; however, it is strongly encouraged that you complete as many major preparation courses as possible prior to transfer.
